blockingIssues.description: The new `RCTNativeBiometricVault.mm` implementation was added under `ios/EnboxMobile/NativeBiometricVault/`, but `ios/EnboxMobile.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj` still has no `NativeBiometricVault` source references, so the TurboModule is not compiled into the app target.

`ios/EnboxMobile/NativeBiometricVault/RCTNativeBiometricVault.mm` maps `errSecAuthFailed` to `AUTH_FAILED` unconditionally, which can misclassify biometry-set invalidation as a retryable auth error instead of the required `KEY_INVALIDATED` recovery path.

`NativeBiometricVaultModule.generateAndStoreSecret()` combines `setRandomizedEncryptionRequired(true)` with a caller-supplied GCM IV during `Cipher.ENCRYPT_MODE`, which can fail for Android Keystore AES/GCM keys and break first-launch secret provisioning.

The default `NativeBiometricVault` Jest mock reports `hasSecret() === false` while `getSecret()` still resolves a secret, so downstream tests can unlock an uninitialized vault and miss real `NOT_FOUND` behavior.

nonBlockingIssues.description: The lifecycle roundtrip test manually flips `hasSecret()` instead of exercising state held by the shipped Jest mock, so it does not catch the inconsistent default-mock behavior.

appliedUpdates.description: Added `.factory/library/native-biometric-vault-platform-gotchas.md` documenting that new iOS Obj-C++ files still need explicit `project.pbxproj` target membership in this repo.

Added `.factory/library/native-biometric-vault-platform-gotchas.md` documenting that Android Keystore AES/GCM keys created with `setRandomizedEncryptionRequired(true)` must use provider-generated IVs on encryption.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Replace the stale ninth canonical biometric error code `TIMEOUT` with `BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT_PERMANENT` so mission guidance matches the native implementations, architecture notes, and validation contract.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `native-biometric-android-impl` and `native-biometric-js-wrapper-and-tests` reviews flagged that `AGENTS.md` still lists `TIMEOUT` while the repo code and other mission docs use `BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT_PERMANENT`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require explicit proof of iOS target registration when new native iOS source files are added, and fail the procedure if the required library reads or `project.pbxproj` inspection are skipped.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The failing `native-biometric-ios-impl` review found the new Obj-C++ file was never added to the Xcode target, and the reviewed transcript skipped both the required library reads and any `project.pbxproj` verification.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Do not allow a feature to remain marked `completed` without a transcript skeleton and handoff artifact for its assigned worker session.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `fix-ci-build-ios` and `native-biometric-android-impl` were reviewed without normal worker artifacts because their assigned sessions timed out or never emitted a handoff, yet the features remained marked completed in mission metadata.

rejectedObservations.observation: `VAL-NATIVE-031` is mapped to the spec-only feature even though the assertion spans later iOS, Android, and Jest files.
Patch functions still silently skip missing targets instead of warning.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in the prior `patch-injection` scrutiny synthesis

rejectedObservations.observation: Workers did not always write failing tests before implementation.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented behavior in prior scrutiny findings, and it did not change the blocking outcome for this milestone

rejectedObservations.observation: The validation contract's Android IV guidance is too permissive for Keystore-backed AES/GCM when randomized encryption is required.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous; captured as a factual library note, but changing the contract text is a normative decision for the orchestrator
